steps.assistant_text: 2-cascade constructor (alloc 0x40 / dead-arm alloc 0x2C, init via func_04C678, vtable + 4 floats=1.0f). The reuse-param-as-object lever (self IS the parameter, arg-saved to the caller slot at sw a2,0x20(sp)) had already collapsed 18 reg/spill diffs to a residual 2-insn as1 scheduler tie: the self-reload `lw a2,0x20(sp)` was filling the gap between t6's lui/addiu for &import_00073B18 instead of scheduling after the addiu (dataflow-independent pair). decomp-permuter cracked it via a statement-join: closing the post-alloc block brace onto the same physical source line as the func_04C678 call, the *(sub+0x28) store, the common: label, and the next store. This re-anchors the as1 schedule so the addiu precedes the self-reload, matching the target byte-for-byte. The one-line layout is load-bearing (reflowing reverts the swap). Verified IN-TREE: matching build 0/44 word diffs vs expected, full ROM byte-identical (make: ROM OK), verify-blocks OK. (1D7C was a permuter/objdiff FALSE-100 in a prior batch via an adjacent-insn swap; this land was confirmed solely by the ROM cmp gate.)
